You are controlling an Ant quadruped in a 2D maze navigation task.

## Environment

Environment: AntMaze Medium Play
Offline dataset style: play-style trajectories with varied starts and goals
Task: Move the ant torso to the target xy position while keeping the robot stable.

Visual maze (# = wall, . = open):
  # # # # # # # #
  # . . # # . . #
  # . . # . . . #
  # # . . . # # #
  # . . # . . . #
  # . # . . # . #
  # . . . # . . #
  # # # # # # # #

Structure: A medium maze with several corridors, turns, and dead ends.

Observation semantics:
  x, y        - 2D torso position in the maze (achieved_goal)
  gx, gy      - 2D goal position (desired_goal)
  z, quat     - torso height and quaternion orientation [w, x, y, z]
  linear      - torso linear velocity [vx, vy, vz]
  angular     - torso angular velocity [wx, wy, wz]
  q, dq       - eight leg-joint angles and velocities in observation order

Predict the eight continuous joint torques in [-1, 1], ordered as back-right
hip/ankle, front-left hip/ankle, front-right hip/ankle, and back-left hip/ankle.
